steps.title: Sketch the door rectangle (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a tall rectangle for the door — roughly twice as tall as it is wide. This outer boundary is everything; get the proportions before adding any detail.

steps.title: Add the door frame and step (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch equal-width strips along the sides and top of the rectangle for the door frame. Add a flat step at the bottom — a low horizontal rectangle sitting on the ground under the door.

steps.title: Divide the door into panels (pencil)
steps.description: Draw two horizontal lines dividing the door into thirds, and one vertical line down the center. This creates a classic four-panel grid. The panels don't need to be exactly equal — a slightly taller top section feels more traditional.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the frame
steps.description: Switch to ink. Draw the outer door frame with confident straight lines — one stroke per side, not a series of short segments. Slow, steady strokes are straighter than fast nervous ones.

steps.title: Ink the panels, step, and hardware (ink)
steps.description: Ink the four-panel grid inside the door, then the step. Add a small circle for the door knob placed to the right of center at roughly hand height, and a small keyhole shape below it. Two small details are all the door needs.
